name: Fortified Cellar, Rue du Moulin
description: A German-improved farmhouse cellar reinforced with railway ties and sandbags, accessed through a trapdoor in the kitchen floor. It served as a company command post and still smells of ersatz coffee, tobacco, and the copper-iron smell of blood from the recent clearing action.
atmosphericDetails: A German field telephone sits on a wooden shelf, its handset off the hook; the wire running from it disappears into the eastern wall through a hole packed with rags
A hand-drawn map of the American artillery positions is pinned to a timber upright with a German bayonet — it is dated May 26th and is accurate to within fifty meters
Spent brass casings crunch underfoot; a German steel helmet sits upright on the floor beside scattered equipment and broken ration tins as if its owner simply set it down and stepped out
